name: Post-readonly Go Scope Assessment Gate
decision: Do not implement any new Go ownership after Phase 19 closure; first create a post-readonly scope assessment and gate future work behind a write/file/migration contract inventory.

python_owns: POST / PUT / DELETE / PATCH on every path

scope_candidates.candidate: Tighten read-only parity before any write ownership
examples: Document exact Caddy matcher in API docs
Lock Python-vs-Go diff coverage for the hardened Caddy production surface
Review whether Go search parity still misses attachment text search behavior
scope_candidates.reason: It improves correctness of the already-promoted surface without expanding runtime ownership.

scope_candidates.candidate: Go ownership for POST/PUT/DELETE /api/notes and note actions
blockers: Requires Note_History and Source_Urls behavior parity
Requires tag/category creation and relation parity
Requires image cleanup behavior for deletes
Requires production DB backup and rollback drill

scope_candidates.candidate: Go ownership for category/tag mutation routes
blockers: Requires category migration of existing notes parity

scope_candidates.candidate: Go ownership for upload, attachment, cleanup, import, or export routes
blockers: Requires upload validation and SSRF guard parity
Requires large rollback plan for file side effects
Server routes are localhost-only operational surface

recommended_path.title: Write Surface Contract Inventory Gate
recommended_path.reason: Before any Go write/file/migration implementation, lock the Python-owned write/file/system surfaces, side effects, rollback requirements, and tests in a machine-readable inventory.
minimum_outputs: Machine-readable route inventory for every Python-owned mutation/file/system route
Side-effect map for DB writes, file writes/deletes, external fetches, process/service actions, and migrations
Backup and rollback requirements per route class
Python-vs-future-Go parity fixtures for candidate write surfaces

not_authorized_by_20_0: Caddy route expansion beyond the validated GET read surface
Changing prism-go-readonly.service mode away from SQLite query_only

result.conclusion: Phase 20 must not start with implementation. The next contract-safe step is a write/file/system route inventory and side-effect map before any Go ownership expansion is considered.

allowed_next_step.title: Write Surface Contract Inventory Gate
allowed_next_step.scope: Plan-only inventory of Python-owned mutation, file, system, import/export, cleanup, and migration surfaces. This does not authorize Go implementation, Caddy expansion, frontend default changes, Python removal, or public exposure.
not_authorized_without_approval: Caddy route expansion beyond the validated GET read surface
